# Transcrição: 04- Handling Objections with Ease

- **Fonte:** 04- Handling Objections with Ease
- **Duração:** 07:38
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

My friends, welcome to the training on objection handling. This is by far one of my favorite parts of the sales conversation. Why? Because it's the part where you get to be a leader. We get to show up for our prospects to help to coach to really be there for them in their time of need when they're in a place that's vulnerable and looking for that inspiration, looking for that empowerment. So first and foremost, objections are not to be feared but embraced. They're obstacles for growth. They're opportunities for us to grow and to transform. So first and foremost, mindset, get yourself ready for objections. Like you want those objections. Now the second thing is that the better you get at your sales conversation, the flow of your sales conversation, the less and less the objections come up because you learn how to pre-handle those objections so they don't even arise in the first place. But if they do, again, no problem. You're there, you're trained, and you're ready to help your prospects out. Lastly, I also want you to reference Tony's training on transformation vocabulary because it's really going to help you with this portion of the sales conversation. So the first thing that we need to figure out when it comes to an objection, a challenge, first off, what is an objection? An objection is, I don't know, it's costly. I don't know if I have the money or my partner, my spouse, my husband, my wife might not like this, I have to talk to them. Anything that's stopping them from being an absolute yes and taking action and signing up with you, that's an objection. Now the first step of what we have to do is we want to really determine is this just like a logistical objection or is this a smoke screen for something else? Now a logistical objection is this is great, but I'm going away on my honeymoon next week and the timing is off. Not a problem. You can usually get around logistical objections just by being creative. Let's do this. Let's schedule our first call for right when you get back. Or let's sign you up now and give you some homework so that when you have downtime, you have something to read through or when you're on the plane, you can usually get through these logistical objections and I'll leave that in your court or your corner. Do not let logistics stop you. Just get creative. However, the majority of our training is focusing on smoke screens. Now what's a smoke screen? 90% of psychology is subconscious, just like the iceberg. And so a lot of times people will give you an objection and they don't even know why they're giving you the objection. Just something feels off and they feel like they just need a little more time to think or they're a little scared or nervous or there could be a whole bunch of things going on. And in that case, that's when we have to step up and serve. So first, we want to just get really clear.

## [03:01 - 06:02]

Is this a logistical thing or is this a smoke screen? So if you're clear this is a smoke screen, then there's three things that we're going to do. Number one, align. What does align mean? align means you never want to confront head on. Well, actually sometimes you do, but more often than not, you never want to confront head on. And if somebody gives you an objection, you don't say you're wrong. No, you align with them because again, you're there to help them and you want to be on their side. So you're going to use language like, I hear you. I can understand that this is a concern for you. This is something that is preventing you from saying yes, I hear you. I understand you. I know you.
